parsed_review.summary: CP00-521 (RP17.P02.M05.S11-S20, risk A, 10 units, micro "Permission And Audit Binding" x10) is a clean, descriptor-only increment that extends the established CP514-CP520 pattern. The pack-level claim is honestly stated as production_ready:false, runtime_ready:false, descriptor_only:true, and that claim is fully supported by the implementation, tests, contract, and recorded validation. Deliverable distribution {ui:2, implementation:3, claude_review:1, failure_recovery:2, test:2} is consistent across prompt, baseline, manifest, requirements, and the validator-enforced counts. Implementation is internally consistent: registry.js defines the CP521 pack binding/requirements/no-write attestation; service.js builds frozen descriptor-only case set + descriptor (all rows descriptor_only:true, runtime_execution:false, customer_safe_errors_only:true, raw_payload_included:false); validators.js provides coverage + slice validators, Hermes/Claude packets and handoff; index.js re-exports transitively via `export *`; the contract is advanced to current_pack CP00-521 with a matching p02_audit_binding_slice_descriptor; and validate-rp17-ai-governance-core-contract.mjs deep-equals contract<->generated descriptor and no_write_attestation while regression-preserving CP514-CP520 and asserting historical_pack_bindings ends at CP00-521. No-write/no-real-data/no-runtime boundaries hold: a grep for I/O (readFile/writeFile/fetch/child_process/exec/spawn/fs/process.env) across packages/ai-governance/src matched only descriptor field names set to false and validator error strings, i.e. zero real I/O, DB, network, or fs writes; source is pure frozen-object construction. The three CP521 tests assert coverage validity, descriptor-only rows, claude_is_final_approval:false, handoff to CP00-522/RP17.P02.M05.S21, and promotes_claude_to_final_approval/claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation:false; node --test recorded exit 0. Claude is consistently constrained to read-only, non-final-approver (request read_only:true with tools Read/Grep/Glob; descriptor and manifest authority boundaries; human_final_approval_required). Hermes patterns are deterministic evidence/receipt-only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false, receipt_shape "...no_write", H17 used as a validation gate; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product:false). CP178+ review-hardening inputs are valid: readiness auth_preflight passed (exit 0, 1455-byte well-formed JSON), validation-report shows forbidden_tools_present:false, prompt_size_ok:true, schema present; no auth failure, 0-byte stdout, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, or missing required fields. The downstream artifacts claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json, raw-output.json, and review-receipt.json are correctly absent at this read_only_claude_run stage (status implemented_pending_review / review pending) and are not defects, and the unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, workbook/*, .docx) are correctly scoped out per changed-file-scope.json. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. This review does not block pack or goal closeout; final pack closeout still legitimately depends on the deferred adjudication/inspection/closeout-pack:validate steps and human final approval, which remain open by design.
